observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to promptly record reconciling items to reflect the accurate balance of the Cash in Bank, LCCA.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the LGU to require the concerned AO to immediately settle or refund the CAs which are already due for liquidation, especially those who have long outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2024; and
b. Maintain updated subsidiary ledgers specifying the nature of the cash advance to aid in tracking and reconciling the balance of the account.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete, updated, properly accomplished and reconciled Stock Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each class of PPE, listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, useful life and other necessary information.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the Due to BIR and to prepare the schedule of the Due to BIR accounts, aside from the subsidiary ledgers, to monitor the outstanding liabilities, particularly for prior years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Local Engineer to implement a sound and effective monitoring system in place to compel contractor’s compliance of the requirements of Section 62 of the RIRR of RA 9184 in order to ensure that the procuring entity will be reimbursed of its expenses in the restoration or reconstruction works in case of structural defect/failure occurring during the applicable warranty period. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor of the above-mentioned projects to submit warranty security.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to require the concerned contractors to strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 relative to the posting of the project signboard to ensure full transparency and accountability in the implementation of the infrastructure projects of the Municipality.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review the Due to NGAs and determine the outstanding balances subject to liquidation or adjustment.

We recommended that the Management:
a) Formulate documented/written guidelines or uniform policy and procedures in the engagement of JO and consultant workers to ensure that the hiring process is systematic and include the standard qualifications based on the work to be assigned;
b) Direct the HRMO together with the department heads/ immediate supervisors to ensure that job orders and consultants accurately reflect a quantified and accurate actual work performed and ensure that these are the actual deliverables stated in their contracts; and
c) Conduct regular assessment of the Manpower Complement of the LGU to determine whether the hiring of a permanent employee is more beneficial than the periodic hiring of job orders or consultants.

We recommended that Management require strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of Purchase Orders and contracts within five days from execution thereof. We likewise recommend that the submission of AIR and request for inspection be made simultaneously with the delivery of foods and other non-food items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the 5% share of the building permit fees and other charges to the DPWH; and
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the liability account for the unremitted share of the 5% share to the DPWH.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the liquidating officers to deposit intact all collections to the authorized depository bank; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to review the remittances and deposits made to/by the Liquidating Officers and implement appropriate actions on the spotted excess of collections against deposits.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to validate and send confirmation letters to various creditors to confirm or determine the exact amount of liability of the LGU;
Ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to various claims before the recording of a liability; and
Exert efforts to locate the documents relative to the long outstanding payable and make the necessary adjusting entries, if there are any.

We recommended that the Management:
To direct the Municipal Development Council to meticulously plan the programs and  projects to be included in the annual  20 percent LDF budget to ensure that they are procurement and implementation ready; and
Execute all the programs and projects within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s programs and projects.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review and reconcile the items listed under Due to NGAs account if they fall within the definition of “liability” and evaluate its appropriate nature of account and  adjust the respective accounts through journal entries;
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Engineering  Office to immediately implement the projects with available funds received from National Government Agencies; and
Immediately liquidate unexpended/excess balance of the fund transfers upon completion of the projects and adjust the Due to NGAs account appropriately.

We recommended that Management:
observe prudence in the procurement of medicines (i.e. multivitamins) charged to DRRMF, taking into consideration the expiration date and the quantity that will mitigate future needs during disasters and calamities; and
Strictly observe proper charging against the Calamity Fund in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ensure that the purpose for the establishment of the fund shall benefit the LGU and its constituents in mitigating and preparedness for calamities that might occur in the future.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the sources, utilization, and unexpended balance of the DRRM Fund in the Notes to FS.

We recommended that Management: 

direct the GSO to update the RPCPPE/IIRUP using the data provided by various offices to reflect the actual count and value of the PPE items maintained by the Municipality and reconcile the same with the accounting records; and 
	
implement the disposal of the unserviceable properties and adjust the PPE in the books of accounts accordingly.

We recommended that Management:

Require the contractor to rectify the deficient measurement in the Constructed Concrete Vehicle Backing Area and/or return the equivalent cost of the deficiency in measurement relative to the project;

 Require the Municipal Engineering Office to inspect thoroughly the project and be particular with the measurement during the actual inspection; and

Require the MEO to accurately execute the detailed engineering, particularly the quantity of measurement to avoid excessive ABC.

We recommended that Management direct the:

 Office of the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPT and SET receivables based on the updated list of real property tax due and collectible to be provided by the MTO and recognize the correct RPT and SET Receivables, if necessary; and	
	
MTO to prepare every month a Summary of Collections and updated RPT/SET due and collectible to aid in the reconciliation of financial records.

We recommended that Management:

adhere to the step-by-step procurement process  provided by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184

We recommended the Management to:

Ensure that taxes withheld are remitted intact and within the period prescribed by pertinent revenue regulations.

We recommended that the Management:

Ensure that the expenditures charged against the GAD Plan and Budget are GAD-related activities and follow the prescribed guidelines and policies of the PCW-DM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; 

Instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report based on the provisions mentioned on the PCW-DM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and

Instruct the GAD Focal Person to maintain a monitoring of all the costs associated with the implementation of GAD PPAs to serve as a basis for the preparation of the GAD AR and establish a GAD database that will include a gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming, and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Management:

instruct the MHO to render a monthly report to the Accounting Office on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside sources; and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Registry of Donated Relief Goods – DRRM, reflect the full cost of ending inventory of goods/vaccines received and issued, and record in the books the cost of donated PPE less its total accumulated depreciation.
42) We recommended that Management consider continuing the works on the municipal covered court to prevent further deterioration and attain optimal use of the facility.

(1) We recommended that the Management of LGU Baao makes representation with the Management of LBP to recover the ₱198,127.49 balance plus interests of said savings accounts from Postal Savings Bank now Overseas Filipino Bank.
observations.reason: The time deposits from Postal Savings Bank now Overseas Filipino Bank amounting to ₱198,127.49 still not recovered. Included in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: (30) We recommended that Management obtain duly signed copies of the MOA for each existing, on-going and un-liquidated fund transfers to which the LGU is a party and henceforth ensure that all concerned offices, especially the offices of the Commission on Audit, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Mayor’s Office be furnished with duly signed MOA for their reference.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
execute all the projects within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects; and
carefully plan the projects/programs/activities to be included in the annual  20 percent LDF budget to ensure that they are procurement and implementation ready.

We recommended that Management:
adhere to the step by step procurement process  provided by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
ensure the provision on start date stated in the Special Conditions Contract are consistent with the provisions of IRR of R.A No. 9184.
We recommended that Management submit all the necessary documents and immediately process the registration of land ownership in the name of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
through the BAC, to specify in the BAC resolution the grounds for  resorting to negotiated procurement under emergency cases; and
resort to other alternative mode of procurement aside from negotiated procurement under emergency cases, when applicable.

We recommended that Management require the:
Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM Officer to strictly adhere to the provision of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and utilize the fund that support disaster risk management activities such as, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery; and
LDRRM Officer to submit complete Distribution Lists for goods and rice distributed to each recipient with complete details as to name, address, number of kilos, purpose and date given.

We recommended that Management immediately return any unused/unexpended balances to the National Treasury or revert it to Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund, if any, and immediately implement the projects.

We recommended that Management require strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of Purchase Orders and contracts within five days from execution thereof.

We recommended that Management

Create a committee composed of one personnel each from General Services and Accounting Office who shall reconcile their respective records of PPE on a full time basis and at the same time maintain updated and complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards.

Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE and prepare RPCPPE with complete details; and

Property Office to update and complete the property cards for all PPE owned by the LGU.
observations.reason:
Partial reconciliation was done by the Inventory Team.  Also, the GSO did not maintain property ledger cards for PPE accounts.


Physical count not conducted for all PPE accounts.  Included in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to require the: 

Human Resource Officer to regularly update any changes of employees’ salaries in the GSIS and Pag-ibig systems and require the Pag-ibig number from JOW; and

Municipal Accountant to settle immediately the unremitted taxes withheld and premiums deducted from the salaries of the employees to avoid any sanctions that may be imposed due to late or non-remittance thereof.

We recommended that Management:

Implement the programs/projects using the fund transfers received from various government agencies, if any;

Submit all the documents requested by the Audit Team related to this fund transfers; and

Review all the unexpended balances of funds received and immediately return any unused/unexpended balances to the National Treasury or revert it to Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund, if there is any.

(18) We recommended that Management require the prompt implementation of projects particularly those funded by various NGAs to effectively and timely extend the benefits of the projects to the constituents.

observations.recommendation: (20) We recommended that Management through the Office of the Municipal Assessor prioritize the updating of its records particularly the Field Appraisal & Assessment Sheet of all real properties in the Municipality so that correct amount of real property taxes may be assessed.
observations.managementAction: Updating of records is on-going

Required information are included in the JOW contracts.





Accomplishment reports submitted by JOW are now quantifiable and traceable to their department’s/section’s performance targets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management obtain duly signed copies of the MOA for each existing, on-going and un-liquidated fund transfers to which the LGU is a party and henceforth ensure that all concerned offices, especially the offices of the Commission on Audit, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Mayor’s Office be furnished with duly signed MOA for their reference.
observations.reason: Copies of MOA were not provided to the Office of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant ensure that the provisions prescribed in COA Circular No. 2007-001 which pertains to the guidelines in the granting, utilization, accounting, and auditing of the funds released to Non-Governmental Organizations/ People’s Organizations be strictly enforced.
observations.reason: BAMVESCO is no longer existing, some of the officers were already dead while some can no longer be located. | Several demand letters were sent to BAMVESCO but to no avail.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management of LGU Baao makes representation with the Management of LBP to recover the ₱198,127.49 balance plus interests of said savings accounts from Postal Savings Bank now Overseas Filipino Bank.

We recommended that Management create a committee composed of one personnel each from General Services and Accounting Office who shall reconcile their respective records of PPE on a full time basis and at the same time maintain updated and complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards.

We recommended that Management require the prompt implementation of projects particularly those funded by various NGAs to effectively and timely extend the benefits of the projects to the constituents.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the fund liquidation report pertaining to the completed projects in order that the account of the Municipality in the books of the source agency is settled.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
require strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of PO and contracts within five (5) days from execution thereof; and
notify the Auditor of the deliveries of procured items within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance in adherence to the provision of COA Cir. No. 95-006.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineering Office to cause the preparation and submission of the above documents for all infrastructure projects implemented by contract and henceforth prepare them religiously and submit the same in time with the requests for payment.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/ projects/ activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and cause the implementation of the projects before the end of the budget year to achieve the purpose for which it was established.
We recommended that Management consider continuing the works on the municipal covered court to prevent further deterioration and attain optimal use of the facility.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting and Property Office to:

 investigate and identify the noted differences between the Accounting records and inventory reports that may require adjustment/s; and 
	



 maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards as required under Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS.
observations.reason:
The variance between the Accounting records and inventory report has substantially increased by ₱23,822,389.17 compared to CY 2018 variance of ₱8,827,687.01.


Incomplete PPELC and Property Cards are maintained by the Accounting and Property Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the overstatement supported with documents evidencing the transfer of the assets to the Department of Education.
We recommended that Management through the Office of the Municipal Accountant refrain from the pre-mature recording of Construction in Progress in the absence of a measurable basis of recording.

We recommended that Management: 

 require the Municipal Accountant to validate the reliability of the recorded balances of fund transfers and refund the unused fund in order that the account of the LGU in the books of the source agencies are settled and/or request for authority to use the savings in the implementation of other related projects; 

 follow up from the source agencies –DAR and DA the release of the remaining amount to be transferred; and 

 ensure that the projects are implemented promptly in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.reason:
Unused funds for completed projects  amounting to ₱407,140.03 is still carried in the books of the LGU


MOA was not submitted to the Audit Team for infra projects funded by national government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the Office of the Municipal Assessor prioritize the updating of its records particularly the Field Appraisal & Assessment Sheet of all real properties in the Municipality so that correct amount of real property taxes may be assessed.
observations.managementAction: Updating of records is on-going

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

 prepare the subsidiary ledgers as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and revert back to the General Fund the unutilized amount for CY 2013; and

 disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the current and previous years’ unutilized balances.

We recommended that Management require the: 

 Human Resource Officer to furnish the Office of the Auditor the certification by the Local Chief Executive/Personnel Officer that the activities/ services to be rendered by the JOWs cannot be provided by regular or permanent personnel of the LGU;  include in JOW contract the functions, duties and responsibilities for each JOW; and evaluate the staffing requirements of each department taking into consideration the available work force provided by permanent employees and the department’s performance targets; 

 Accounting Office and Human Resource Office to ensure that required supporting documents for overtime claims are attached in the payroll and 

 JOWs to provide accomplishment reports which are quantifiable and traceable to their department’s/section’s performance targets.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/ projects/ activities to be funded out of the 20% LDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, 34 projects or 48 per cent of key programs and projects under the 20% LDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider implementing projects by straight contract to ensure its 100 percent accomplishment as planned and not delay the immediate utilization of the projects by the beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the KALAHI office and furnish the latter with the list of barangays and their respective un-liquidated local counterparts so that municipal records may be updated.  We further recommend that a provision be added to the Agreement which would require KALAHI as the implementing agency to submit a liquidation report to all parties in the MOA within a specified number of days after project completion.

We recommended that Management:

require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to determine/investigate the cause/s of the discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments in their respective records; and
require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain Property, Plant & Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for each category as required under the NGAs for LGUs.
observations.reason:
Physical count of PPE was conducted, however, the inventory report submitted and the accounting records showed a difference of ₱8.827 million.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management obtain duly signed copies of the MOA for each existing, on-going and un-liquidated fund transfers to which the LGU is a party and henceforth ensure that all concerned offices, especially the offices of the Commission on Audit, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Mayor’s Office be furnished with duly signed MOA for their reference.
observations.reason: Copies of the MOA for fund transferred by national government agencies were not provided to the Office of the Auditor, hence compliance by the LGU to the MOA on the implementation of the PPA cannot be validated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/ projects/ activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1.  We likewise recommend that Management implement its projects using straight contract to boost 20% DF projects implementation.
observations.reason:
For CY 2018, 39 projects budgeted at ₱37.16 million were not implemented.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Accountant ensure that the provisions prescribed in COA Circular No. 2007-001 which pertains to the guidelines in the granting, utilization, accounting, and auditing of the funds released to Non-Governmental Organizations/ People’s Organizations be strictly enforced.
observations.managementAction: Several demand letters were sent to BAMVESCO but to no avail.
observations.reason: BAMVESCO is no longer existing, some of the officers were already dead while some can no longer be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accounting and Property Office to: a) investigate and identify the noted differences between the Accounting records and inventory reports that may require adjustment/s; and b) maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards as required under Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the overstatement supported with documents evidencing the transfer of the assets to the DepEd.
We recommended that Management through the Office of the Municipal Accountant refrain from the pre-mature recording of Construction in Progress in the absence of a measurable basis of recording.

We recommended that Management: a) require the Municipal Accountant to validate the reliability of the recorded balances of fund transfers and refund the unused fund in order that the account of the LGU in the books of the source agencies are settled and/or request for authority to use the savings in the implementation of other related projects; b) follow up from the source agencies –DAR and DA the release of the remaining amount to be transferred; and c) ensure that the projects are implemented promptly in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Management through the Office of the Municipal Assessor prioritize the updating of its records particularly the Field Appraisal & Assessment Sheet of all real properties in the Municipality so that correct amount of real property taxes may be assessed.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to: a) prepare the subsidiary ledgers as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and revert back to the General Fund the unutilized amount for CY 2013; and b) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the current and previous years’ unutilized balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the: a.) Human Resource Officer to furnish the Office of the Auditor the certification by the Local Chief Executive/Personnel Officer that the activities/ services to be rendered by the JOWs cannot be provided by regular or permanent personnel of the LGU; include in JOW contract the functions, duties and responsibilities for each JOW; and evaluate the staffing requirements of each department taking into consideration the available work force provided by permanent employees and the department’s performance targets; b.) Accounting Office and Human Resource Office to ensure that required supporting documents for overtime claims are attached in the payroll and c.) JOWs to provide accomplishment reports which are quantifiable and traceable to their department’s/section’s performance targets.
We recommended that Management ensure that expenses charged against the SEF were in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 2017.
observations.reason: No similar observation was noted for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Agency require the Solid Waste Management Board, the Municipal Planning Office and all the Departments involve in planning and conceptualizing the Solid Waste Management Plan to give their full attention and cooperation to comply with whatever requirement of the National Solid Waste Management Commission for the approval of the LGU’s SWMP.  We likewise recommend that the Local Chief Executive consider the strict implementation of Municipal Ordinance No. 2011-056, specifically Sections 11 and 12, to instill discipline among its constituents and make waste segregation at source a habit.
observations.reason: The Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU was already prepared and submitted to the DILG for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed give priority to the creation of a functional LCPC in accordance with the requirement of the law and provide necessary appropriation for its PPAs to ensure proper coordination and assistance in the adoption and implementation of comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention.
observations.reason: The Local Chief Executive issued Executive Order No. 2018-011 on May 15, 2018 creating the LCPC and defining its functions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider implementing projects by straight contract to ensure its 100 percent accomplishment as planned and not delay the immediate utilization of the projects by the beneficiaries.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, several infrastructure projects were already through straight contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) require the Municipal Accountant to record the applicable receivables for advance payment and prior years’ collectibles to properly adjust the amount previously booked up in RPT Receivable/Deferred RPT and SET Receivable/Deferred SET; adjust the receivables and deferred income for the collections of current year advance payment and prior years’ tax due to properly reflect the balance of RPT Receivable/ Deferred RPT and SET Receivable and /Deferred SET; reconcile the balance of /SETR and DRPT/DSET against total delinquent as of December 2016; and (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to forward to the Office of the Municipal Accountant all documents attached to all remittances for reference in recording in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the KALAHI office and furnish the latter with the list of barangays and their respective un-liquidated local counterparts so that municipal records may be updated.  We further recommend that a provision be added to the Agreement which would require KALAHI as the implementing agency to submit a liquidation report to all parties in the MOA within a specified number of days after project completion.
observations.managementAction: Some of the completed projects have already been recorded.
observations.reason: Regional Office of the DSWD has not yet forwarded the liquidation report of some of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Accountant ensure that the provisions prescribed in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 be strictly enforced.

Aside the allocation for QRF - and 70% for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded, it shall-also- include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and (b) direct  the  Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance  of LDRRMF -  MOOE amounting to P3,486,834.81 fiom the General Fund to the Trust Fund account.

observations.managementAction: Representation and Miscellaneous Expense is not compliant with the limits set forth in RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider other modes of project implementation such as by contract in order to maximize the benefits expected which could be derived from the 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: Management through the BAC has already lined-up projects for bidding. Implementation by contract is expected to begin CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
The Team recommended that the Management: () prioritize the immediate repair of the equipment needed so that residual WMRF will be immediately eliminated; and (b) hand in hand with the barangay officials, continue to conduct education-and dissemination campaign on proper waste management.

Finally, to document and to have a reliable tool in validation and verification of the effectiveness of the program and reference for future plans, we recommended that the quantity of garbage colection, produced organic fertilizers and by products of non-‘biodegradables must likewise be monitored.
observations.managementAction: Management immediately had the equipment repaired.
observations.reason: WMRF will be immediately eliminated; and education and dissemination campaign to continue.

observations.managementAction: Management is now compliant with the rules and regulations in the disbursement of funds.

after  proper  inspection
require the  Municipal
Accountant to immediately
reclassify these
unserviceable PPE  to
Other  Assets Account to
enable other concerned
Officers to proceed with
the sale and destruction,
whichever is applicable, of
these assets following the
provision of Section 79 of
PD 1445,
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014
observations.reason: Instead of booking up to Other Assets these will be reported in the disposal in compliance with PPSAS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Management start
classifying small tangible
items as Inventories instead
of PPE in accordance with
the provisions of COA
Circular 2005-002 dated
April 14, 2005 and using
Annex A as their guide.
These items, since already
issued, should be adjusted
to Prior Years Adjustment
as it understate PY's
expense.

Lastly, require concemed
officers, upon issuance of
Inventory items, to prepare
an Inventory Custodian
Slip to ensure proper
monitoring of these assets.
observations.reason: Inventory of Land was conducted. Restated in Part I of this Report,

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management to:

a. Locate for  the
Contract to Sell issued
to various land on
instalment and review
the embodied terms
and conditions in the
contract.  Since the
contract has been long
overdue and payment
has not been made,
and if the condition
warrants,  rescinding
the contract may be
considered,

b. For  untitled
untransferred
properties, review the
documents  proving
their ownership of the
property and allocate
amount  for  the
immediate transfer of
these property in the
name of the LGU; and

c. Finally prepare an
assessment to
determine the amount
of the properties and
prepare the necessary —
adjustment in books.
observations.reason: Inventory of Land was conducted. Restated in Part I of this Report,

observations.recommendation:
To avoid disallowance in
audit and to prevent
misspending and/or wastage of government
funds, the Municipality is
highly enjoined to abide
strictly with the guidelines
for the prevention and
disallowance of IUEEU set
forth by COA Circular No.
2012-003,
observations.reason: Gathering of documents from the concerned DEPEd officials takes time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the
Office of the Municipal
Accountant ensure that the
provisions prescribed in
COA Circular No. 2007-
001 which pertains to the
guidelines in the granting,
utilization, accounting, and
auditing of the funds
released to Non-
Governmental
Organizations/  People’s
Organizations be strictly
enforced.
